meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Setup: Debugging Guide for Student AI Builders
content.seo_description: Master debugging with this Claude Code setup checklist. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude CLI to fix errors and build your portfolio faster.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at cryptic stack traces. By integrating Claude Code into your local environment, you turn your terminal into an elite pair-programming partner. Use Antigravity to architect your logic, then let Claude Code autonomously identify, test, and patch your bugs, transforming frustrating roadblocks into rapid learning milestones.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your code refuses to run. You’ve spent hours hunting a semicolon or a logic flaw, and the imposter syndrome is starting to creep in. Every developer hits this wall, but the difference between a frustrated student and a ship-ready engineer is the ability to leverage AI-native debugging workflows.

Today, we bridge that gap. We are moving beyond copy-pasting errors into a browser. We are installing a professional-grade debugging engine that lives directly in your terminal, allowing you to iterate at the speed of thought. Let’s turn your coding environment into a high-performance laboratory.

steps.title: Configure Claude Code CLI
steps.description: Install the Claude Code CLI to provide your terminal with direct access to your local codebase, enabling autonomous file analysis and modification.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project directory structure and identify potential bottlenecks in the authentication flow.

steps.title: Establish Context with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to map out your architecture so Claude understands your intent before it starts modifying files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-level technical specification for this feature. Include edge cases for error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --read-only "Review my architecture spec against current file logic."

steps.title: Automated Error Trace Analysis
steps.description: Pipe your build errors directly into Claude Code to get pinpoint explanations instead of generic stack overflow threads.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain this stack trace in the context of my project architecture and suggest the specific line causing the memory leak.
steps.claude_code_command: claude "Analyze the last 50 lines of my build log and propose a fix."

steps.title: Refactoring for Maintainability
steps.description: Once the bug is squashed, use Claude Code to clean up the technical debt created during the initial build phase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this module to follow clean code principles while maintaining existing functionality.
steps.claude_code_command: claude "Refactor the user-auth module to use async/await patterns instead of callbacks."

steps.title: Verification and Unit Testing
steps.description: Ensure your fix is robust by having Claude write and run unit tests against the fixed code block.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of Jest unit tests that cover the success and failure paths for this function.
steps.claude_code_command: claude "Run the new test suite and verify the fix passes all edge cases."

pro_tips: Always commit your code to Git before running Claude Code CLI commands so you can perform a hard reset if the AI hallucinates a solution.
Use the '--read-only' flag in Claude Code when exploring legacy codebases to prevent accidental destructive changes during analysis.
Create a '.clauderc' file in your project root to provide context-specific instructions, such as 'always use TypeScript' or 'enforce functional programming'.
If Claude gets stuck in a loop, interrupt the process and provide a specific architectural constraint from your Antigravity documentation to re-anchor it.
Treat Claude Code as a junior developer; review every proposed 'diff' carefully before hitting the 'y' key to apply changes.
content.next_steps: Now that your debugging engine is active, pick a feature you previously abandoned due to bugs and apply these steps to finish it. Once you have a working prototype, document your process in a README to showcase your problem-solving skills to potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with a professional-grade debugging stack. Don't let a single error stop your momentum. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
